feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Cannot deploy CAP application in SAP BTP Trial – stuck at "Deploying the Application"
entry.content:
Hello,

I am following this SAP Learning course using a SAP BTP Trial account:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/develop-extensions-with-cap-following-the-sap-btp-developer-s-guide/deploying-the-application-1

I am currently stuck at the "Deploying the Application" step.

When I try to deploy the application, the deployment fails and does not complete successfully.

My environment:
- SAP BTP Trial account
- SAP Business Application Studio
- CAP project

I followed the tutorial steps, but I still get errors during deployment.


Is this issue related to Trial account limitations, outdated tutorial steps, or environment settings?

Could you please advise how to resolve this issue or how to troubleshoot it?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Select the Most Appropriate Fiori Elements Floorplant (or Free Style)
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

I have the following scenario for developing a Fiori app using the CAP framework (NodeJS) on BTP.

The app needs to display various information about a Production Batch: general batch information, its list of items, and also allow the user to capture weights taken from an electronic scale connected to the system.

Initially, I'm leaning towards an Object Page, with its header and several sections. Perhaps even a custom section with Fiori Element extensions for weight capture.

My question stems from the following: How would the user reach such Object Page? Up until now, I've always used an Object Page as part of the List Report - Object Page pattern. However, in the scenario of this application I need to build, the user wouldn't reach the Object Page after searching for/selecting an item from a list. Instead, the user scans (with an electronic scanner) a label that has a barcode (or QR code), which contains the batch ID that needs to be displayed/processed. In other words, what should the homepage look like?

I've considered an object page with no content (but I'm not sure if that's possible) or a very simple freestyle page with an input field and a "Go" button or something similar (that activates on scan and navigates to the object page).

However, since I have several ideas but nothing concrete, I've decided to ask the community here. Is there a more appropriate pattern or approach for this application scenario?

Thanks in advance for any answers and/or suggestions.

entry.title: Unable to pass filter parameter from the URL of CAPM application generated with Fiori List Rep. Page
entry.content:
I am developing a SAP BTP CAP OData V4 Fiori Elements List Report (Standalone) application and attempting to pass filter values via the URL.

In my service definition, you created:

  • An entity ldata

  • A projection entity lDataCopy(based on lData)

REQUIREMENT:

 I want to embed this as mashup in Sales and Service Cloud V2 inside account. So when user click on account it loads the details of that particular account only.

Problem

The filter parameters passed in the input URL (for example: as shown below) are not being applied in the List Report.

index.html?status=02          OR           index.html?$filter=status eq '02'

Current Behavior

  1. The application loads successfully.
  2. The filter value provided in the URL is not reflected in the Smart Filter Bar.
  3. The List Report does not apply the filter automatically.
  4. But if I apply filter in the filter bar and press GO, it works fine.

Expected Behavior

  1. URL query parameters should map automatically to @ui.SelectionFields.
  2. The corresponding filter field should be prefilled.
  3. The List Report should execute the search with the provided filter value.

Please help

entry.title: How to send email with attachments through SAP BTP Job Scheduler service
entry.content:
We have created action that the job scheduler triggers to send mail in CAPM and We are receiving email through SAP BTP Job Scheduler service without any attachments and getting success message.

When we attach any files and try to send the email using same action that the job scheduler triggers, we are getting success message and job scheduler log is also success, no code level error but email not received.

Created an application/service (CAP, Node.js)

This service will:

  • Generate or fetch the attachment (PDF, CSV, Excel, etc.) from Object Store on SAP BTP
  • Compose an email
  • Send the email using:
    • SAP BTP Destination (SMTP)
    • External mail provider (SendGrid)
Can SAP Job Scheduler send an attachment?

entry.title: The JWT token only contains the openid scope, but none of the custom application scopes
entry.content:
Hi,

I am building CAP application and try to add custom roles and rolecollections through xs-security.json. Deploying in CF. 

When i deploy my xs-auth service, i can see roles and rolecollections being created, i can see them in BTP cockpit Rolecollections, Roles . But what is missing is, i cant see roles under rolecollections. I tried below -

1) Created just roles through xs-security.json and created rolecollections manually in BTP. when try to browse and attach roles to rolecollection, i cant find my roles, templates or application identifier.

2) Created both roles and rolecollections through xs-security.json - i cant see roles inside rolecollections.

3) Removed template $XSAPPNAME from "role-template-references" as per recommendations, but i cant deploy, it gives error.

4) I tried delete and recreated whole auth service with new template, application name, identifier... no luck.

I am getting authenticated, but authorization is failing. My application require annotations and actions to be controlled (who can press button and perform action, this require custom roles). 

Attached template i am using. I am sure you might have similar situations before. Please let me know what is the best way to resolve this issue. As Always, thanks for your help.

Below is my app trace log.

template used as below:

{
"xsappname": "my-app-reprocess-v2",
"tenant-mode": "dedicated",
"description": "Message Reprocessing Application - Security Configuration",
"scopes": [
{
"name": "$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer",
"description": "View messages, content, and statistics (read-only access)"
},
{
"name": "$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessAdministrator",
"description": "Full administrative access including reprocess operations and CRUD"
}
],
"attributes": [
{
"name": "email",
"description": "User email address",
"valueType": "string"
}
],
"role-templates": [
{
"name": "ReprocessViewer",
"description": "Read-Only Access - View messages and statistics",
"scope-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer"
],
"attribute-references": [
"email"
]
},
{
"name": "ReprocessAdministrator",
"description": "Full Administrative Access - CRUD operations and reprocess actions",
"scope-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessAdministrator",
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer"
],
"attribute-references": [
"email"
]
}
],
"role-collections": [
{
"name": "MessageReprocessViewersRC",
"description": "Message Reprocess Viewers - Read-only access",
"role-template-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessViewer"
]
},
{
"name": "MessageReprocessAdministratorsRC",
"description": "Message Reprocess Administrators - Full access",
"role-template-references": [
"$XSAPPNAME.ReprocessAdministrator"
]
}
],
"oauth2-configuration": {
"credential-types": ["binding-secret", "x509"],
"redirect-uris": [
"https://*.cfapps.example.com/**",
"http://localhost:*/**"
],
"token-validity": 3600,
"refresh-token-validity": 86400
}
}

entry.title: Blue Green Deployment - Cannot bind "idle" app to BTP Object Store
entry.content:
I'm trying to switch from regular deployments to blue green deployments using the "cf deploy --strategy blue green" setting. This deploys our CAP app twice, once as "live" and once as "idle". The live app is already bound to the BTP Object Store via a service binding. The deployment tries to bind the new "idle" application now to the same Object Store instance as well but fails with the following message:

Binding service instance "object-store" to application "my-backend-idle"...
Async operation for service binding "9920bf33-8100-4aa9-9829-f687e5869ad4" between app "my-backend-idle" and service instance "object-store" 
with offering "objectstore" and plan "standard" failed with "10009 CF-UnableToPerform bind could not be completed: 

Service broker error: Service broker object-storage-broker failed with: 
Cannot create anymore bindings for service instance 0130aae9-1217-41a7-a864-405e2c74cf2c. 
The limit on number of bindings 5, that can be created for each serviceInstance has been reached. 
If more binding credentials are required, please unbind one of your applications or make use of cf service keys concept. 
Details on how to use service keys can be found in user documentation."

A step of the process has failed. Retrying it may solve the issue.
Process failed.

Currently, there is only one application bound to the service instance, and it still fails with the error message that a max of 5 apps can be bound. Even when checking directly with cf curl "/v3/service_credential_bindings?service_instance_guids=<id> I can only see the existing bound app and no other bindings. According to the service documentation, it should be possible to bind 5 apps, so I'm not sure why it thinks the limit is reached here when it's clearly not. Is there any way to "reset" this without having to delete the whole service instance? I already tried removing the existing binding and rebinding, but the second binding always fails with the same message... Any help would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: CAP Transport Management On Prem System
entry.content: Hi Just a Simple Question i want to make a simple application where user will just give the TR number and why to import it and if i approve it it will auto import it into till production so i want to use CAP and change management how can i use can someone give me an approach that i can follow for this and it should also support the on prem system.

entry.title: getting no runnable resources in bas when try to create run configuration for capm application,
entry.content: getting no runnable resources in bas when try to create run configuration for capm application, check the scn, didnt find any answer, an old thread of 2021 stated that issue was fixed, can someone pls help

entry.title: Support Channels for CDS compiler and cds-maven-plugin
entry.content:
Hi,

if I follow the suggestions from https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/resources/#support-channels , I land in the "Get Support" (create a case) section of SAP for me, where I find no field for the product code provided on the resources page but instead have a required "System" field. Since my issue involves an unexpected CdsMojo Error when updating @sap/cds from 9.6.4 to 9.7.2, there is no SAP System involved.

It seems the recommended support channel does no longer support CDS cases? Or am I missing something? How do we get support for cds, cds compiler and cds-maven-plugin?

Kind regards, Claus

entry.title: How to Pass a Filter Parameter to OData V4 TreeTable Hierarchy Batch Calls in Fiori Elements?
entry.content:
I am building a Fiori Elements List Report page with a TreeTable using the sap.fe.templates.ListReport template and OData V4. The table uses SAP Hierarchy annotations (com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1) with a hierarchyQualifier.

My manifest configuration looks like this:

"controlConfiguration": {
    "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem": {
        "tableSettings": {
            "type": "TreeTable",
            "hierarchyQualifier": "ResponseHierarchy",
            "selectionMode": "None"
        }
    }
}

The Problem

When the page loads, Fiori Elements automatically fires 3 batch calls:

GET ResponseHierarchy(db05dc96-ea30-4393-a929-266e1c8d2331)
GET ResponseHierarchy/$count
GET ResponseHierarchy?$apply=com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1.TopLevels(
    HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy,
    HierarchyQualifier='ResponseHierarchy',
    NodeProperty='ID',
    Levels=1
)&$select=...&$count=true&$skip=0&$top=240

I need to pass a programCycleId to all 3 calls so my backend handler knows which program's data to fetch. The first call works fine because the ID is part of the key segment. However, calls 2 and 3 hit the entity set directly ($root/ResponseHierarchy) without any key or filter.

What I Have Tried

1. Passing ID as route key

{ "pattern": "ResponseHierarchy({ID}):?query:" }

This works for call 1 but calls 2 and 3 don't carry the ID.

2. Passing $filter as query parameter

oParams = { query: { "$filter": "programCycleId eq '" + sProgramId + "'" } };

The $filter in the route URL is not propagated to the $apply=TopLevels(...) and $count calls. The TreeTable control generates these calls referencing $root/ResponseHierarchy directly, completely ignoring the URL $filter.

3. Custom HTTP Header

I ended up using a workaround, setting a custom header x-program-cycle-id on the OData model and reading it in the backend:

oModel.changeHttpHeaders({ "x-program-cycle-id": sProgramCycleId });

This works but it's not ideal because:

  • The header is set globally on the model, affecting all other OData calls
  • It requires manual cache busting (getAllBindings().filter(...).forEach(b => b.refresh()))
  • It's not a clean OData-native solution

My Question

Is there a proper OData V4 / Fiori Elements native way to pass a filter or parameter that gets included in ALL 3 TreeTable hierarchy batch calls, including the $apply=TopLevels(HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy...) call?

Specifically I would like to know:

  1. Is it possible to configure the TreeTable binding via annotations so that a $filter is always appended to all hierarchy calls including the $root/ResponseHierarchy reference inside $apply?

  2. Is there a manifest.json setting or UI5 Fiori Elements API that allows scoping the hierarchy data to a specific filter value without resorting to custom headers?

  3. Is the custom header approach actually the recommended workaround for this scenario, or is there a better pattern?

Any guidance or pointers to documentation would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: It is possible to deploy two CAP Apps to the same PostgreSQL database in Cloud Foundry?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Me and my colleagues are new to CAP development, we are using tutorials and the documentation as source for our first steps. We were able already to create and deploy one CAP App on Cloud Foundry, connected to one PosgtreSQL Instance, which is working like a charm.

However now we need to build a second app, which has a different schema.cds file. We built it, tested it locally (in memory database), but when we try to deploy it, we always receive one error related to a DROP TABLE command.

[PollExecuteTaskStatusExecution] 09...e [2026-03-04T15:47:40.269285] /> deployment to postgres-3...ws.com:8501 failed (STDOUT, APP/TASK/deploy-to-postgresql)#
[PollExecuteTaskStatusExecution] 09...ee [2026-03-04T15:47:40.270089] Error: CDS compilation failed (@sap/cds-compiler v6.7.3) (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-to-postgresql)#
#2026 03 04 15:47:44.785#Z#DEBUG#43...-postgres-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-33947#
[PollExecuteTaskStatusExecution] 093...e [2026-03-04T15:47:40.270114] Error: Dropping tables is not supported (in artifact:“db.BusinessCaseHeaderConfig”) (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-to-postgresql)#

 

The db.BusinessCaseHeaderConfig is actually one table of our other app.

As a workaround for this, we added at the mta.yaml, at the cap service configuration the following PG configuration:

    properties:
      PGOPTIONS: -c search_path=workflowconfig,public
 
In this way, we force our app to use a different schema, which we had to create manually at the PostgreSQL Instance.
 
I would like to know if is there any other way to do the deployment of two CAP Apps, with different schemas into the same PostgreSQL and schema?
Does anyone has experience with that? Is that even possible?

We saw that the content of the table cds_model is what contain the model information, we think that here is the problem, this table only accepts one line, therefore only one app.

BR
Eduardo

entry.title: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on parent entity field value in CAP JAVA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a CAP JAVA based oData service, which is composed on a parent and its child entities. Now, we have a requirement to add a Value Help for one of the child entity field. I have a issue here I want to filter records in this Value help on the basis of a field value from its parent entity.

Could you please help?

Thanks,
Shivani

entry.title: Pass unnamed inbound parameter to a CAP CDS action
entry.content:
Dear community,

I am trying to build a CAP CDS service where it would be helpful if I could benefit from a custom-defined type as an inbound action parameter type in BUT without the need to specify the parameter name. For example:  

type Type1 
 {acknowledge: String;
      message: String; 
  }

  action Action1 (Type1 ) returns String;

Do you think it is possible?

Best regards

entry.title: Reload an Object Page with a different record after a custom action
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

I am working on a SAP CAP app (NodeJS) with SAP Fiori Elements on SAP BTP. At the SAP Fiori Elements level I am also using some Extension.

What I want to do is simple to explain: After executing certain custom action on an Object Page, I need to reload that page, but with changing the key, to display another object, i.e. another record of the same entity... For example, the Object Page is displaying Batch 10001, and after the custom action, the object page should reload and display Batch 10015.

How could I do this?

Thanks in advance for any help and/or suggestions.

entry.title: @sap/cap-rfc update to use a RFC websocket destination
entry.content: Will there be an update to the @sap/cap-rfc library to use a destination from the btp destination service, that is configured as RFC Websocket, since the Cloud Connector 2.19 allows such connections? This would make it easier for applications to use secured rfc connections with principal propagation without configuring SNC.

entry.title: Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigates to transient $uid path instead of server draft path,
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing an issue in a CAP + Fiori Elements OData V4 draft application

Problem:

 When I press Create in one specific config app (AggrConfigService / Aggregator), the draft creation flow breaks.

The UI navigates/binds to a transient client-side path like: AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)

CAP then returns: 404 Invalid resource path "AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=...)" What is strange is that the POST itself succeeds first.

Observed request/response

1) Create request: POST Aggregator Body: {"AggregatorID":"Test"}

Response: HTTP/1.1 204 No Content location: Aggregator.drafts('Test') 2) Immediately after that, UI5 sends: GET Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)?$select=AggregatorID,AggregatorName,HasActiveEntity,HasDraftEntity,IsActive,IsActiveEntity&$expand=DraftAdministrativeData($select=DraftIsCreatedByMe,DraftUUID,InProcessByUser) Response: HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found {"error":{"code":"404","message":"Invalid resource path \"AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)\""}}

So the backend seems to create the draft successfully and returns a draft location, but the frontend still tries to read via the transient $uid path instead of switching to the canonical draft path.

Environment:

SAP CAP Node.js - @sap/cds 8.9.8 - SAPUI5 1.142.0 - Fiori Elements OData V4 - Draft-enabled service - Cloud Foundry deployment on BTP

Questions:

1) Has anyone seen Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigate to a transient `$uid` path like this after a successful POST?

2) Is there any known issue/pattern where FE object-page routing or binding keeps the transient context instead of resolving to the canonical draft location returned by CAP?

Thanks a lot.

Best Regards,

Muthu

SAPUI5 SAP Fiori Elements SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  OData SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: PostgreSQL Schema Evolution in SAP CAP - How to Handle DROP and RENAME Operations
entry.content:
I've been working with SAP CAP and @cap-js/postgres , and discovered a critical gap in schema evolution capabilities. CAP's schema_evolution: 'alter' mode intentionally avoids destructive operations (DROP, RENAME) for safety reasons, but this creates real operational challenges in production deployments.

The problem: When you need to remove a column or rename a table, CAP won't handle it automatically. This forces either:
- Manual SQL scripts (error-prone)
- Database downtime for deployments
- Schema drift issues

I've created a solution called @cds-pgroll/postgres  that bridges this gap. It runs as a pre-deployment step using pgroll (inspired by Shopify's approach) to safely handle destructive operations before CAP's standard schema sync.

Key features:
- Automatic CSN delta detection (compares deployed vs. new schema)
- Supports: DROP table/column, RENAME table/column, add constraints
- 2-phase deployment pipeline with Cloud Foundry MTA integration
- Zero-downtime migrations for most operations

If anyone has faced similar challenges with CAP schema evolution, or has feedback on this approach, I'd love to hear your experiences and use cases.

GitHub: https://github.com/Medelkouch98/cds-pgroll
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cds-pgroll/postgres

entry.title: 401 Unauthorized when sending notifications from CAP using SAP_Notifications destination
entry.content:
I am creating a CAP application to send notifications and configured the SAP_Notifications destination according to the documentation below.

When I call the API using Postman, it works correctly, but when I try to call it from my application, it always returns 401 Unauthorized.

I also tried calling it directly using the SAP Cloud SDK, following this documentation: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/developing-cloud-foundry-applications-with-notifications

Documentation used to configure the destination:
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/enabling-notifications-for-custom-apps-on-sap-btp-cloud-foundry#configure-the-destination-to-the-notifications-service

Additionally, I tried using @cap-js/notifications, but I always receive 401, both locally and after deployment.

Does anyone know what could be causing this or how to fix it?

Additional information:

  • Destination configuration screenshot

  • Postman request working screenshot

The configurations are the same as the ones generated in Work Zone for the notification service.

The BTP logs show a 401 Unauthorized error during the content deployment process in BTP.

If anyone has faced this issue before, I would appreciate any guidance.

entry.title: Running Hugging Face AI Models with SAPUI5 and CAPM
entry.content:
Hi everyone 👋

After spending several hours researching and experimenting, I finally found a simple way to answer a question that many SAP developers have started asking:

“Is it possible to run AI models inside SAP applications using SAPUI5 and CAPM?”

The short answer is:

   Yes, absolutely!

In this blog, I will share how we can integrate Hugging Face AI models with SAPUI5 and CAPM, and how we can build a simple AI-powered application that can generate text responses and even images.

 

                                                                        Fig 1.1 Hugging Face Models sections

Now the Interesting Part: Can SAPUI5 Use AI?

        When I started this experiment, my first question was:

Can SAPUI5 directly call an AI model?

        The answer is yes, especially for text generation models.

To make this work, we only need three things:

- A Hugging Face account
-  An HF API Token
-  A model endpoint

Once you log in to Hugging Face, you can generate an HF Token from your account settings.

Then you can choose any model depending on your use case. Hugging Face provides model benchmarks and descriptions, so you can easily decide which one is best for:

  • Education
  • Coding
  • Reasoning
  • Chat
  • Content generation

Connecting Hugging Face with SAPUI5

Now let's talk about the implementation.

In the SAPUI5 controller, we can send a request to the Hugging Face API using JavaScript fetch().

The UI simply takes a user prompt, sends it to the AI model, and displays the response.

Example flow:

User enters a prompt:

       What is Hugging Face and SAPUI5?

SAPUI5 sends the prompt to the Hugging Face model.

The model generates a response and returns it.

Then SAPUI5 displays the result directly on the UI.

This makes it very easy to build AI chat assistants inside SAP applications.

 

                                                                                    Fig 1.2 Output- Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

Sample Code:

              Fig 1.3 sapui5 Controller.js - Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

                      Fig 1.3 sapui5 view.xml - Hugging Face Ai Chat

But What About Image Generation?

      This is where things become interesting.

      When I tried running image generation models directly in SAPUI5, I realized something important.

      SAPUI5 works best with text-based AI APIs.

      For image generation models, we need a backend service.

This is where CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model) becomes very useful.

 

Using CAPM for Image Generation

To generate images, we can integrate fal.ai, which provides inference APIs for advanced image generation models.

Some examples include:

  • black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev
  • Tongyi-MAI / Z-Image-Turbo

These models can generate high-quality AI images from text prompts.

For example:

"Portrait of a futuristic astronaut with glowing blue eyes"

The system sends this prompt to the AI model, and the model generates an image.

 

How CAPM Handles This

Inside the CAPM Node.js service, we create a custom action such as:

     generateImage

This action receives the prompt from SAPUI5.

Then CAPM calls the fal.ai API with that prompt.

The AI model generates the image and returns a URL of the generated image, which we send back to the UI.

This makes CAPM act as a bridge between SAPUI5 and AI models.

 

Why Use fal.ai Instead of Hugging Face Directly?

You might ask:

If we already use Hugging Face, why do we need fal.ai?

   Good question.

Some models listed on Hugging Face are actually hosted through different inference providers, including fal.ai.

For example:

"black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev"

This model is often executed through fal.ai inference APIs.

So fal.ai becomes an easy way to run those models with strong GPU performance.

Sample Code:

                   Fig 1.4 Output – CAPM - Hugging face Image generation

 

 

                  Fig 1.5 service.js - first half- Hugging face Image generation

 

                  Fig 1.6 service.js - second half - Hugging face Image generation

 

               Fig 1.7 Fal.ai Api key Generation - Hugging face Image generation

 

Final Result

After connecting everything together, we get a simple AI application:

The application can:

✅Generate AI text responses using SAPUI5
✅Generate AI images using CAPM services
✅Use Hugging Face models
✅Run without training your own model

All of this can be done using free developer APIs, unless you need large-scale GPU workloads.

 

Conclusion

From this experiment, we can see that SAP applications can easily integrate with modern AI platforms.

The architecture becomes very simple:

                                         SAPUI5
                                              ⬇
                                  CAPM Service
                                             ⬇
                    Hugging Face / fal.ai AI Models

 

Using this approach, developers can build powerful applications such as:

  • AI chat assistants inside SAP
  • AI-powered knowledge systems
  • Image generation tools
  • Smart enterprise automation

And the best part is that you can even deploy your own AI model on Hugging Face and use it inside SAP applications.

 

⭐I hope this small experiment helps other developers who are curious about AI + SAP integration.

Happy coding! 🚀

entry.title: CAP CDS: customize error response structure before OPEN API generation
entry.content:
Hi community,

I trying to generate an OpenAPI documentation from a CAP CDS service. I was wondering if there is any way to influence the Error Response structure before generation (an annotation maybe).

Any hints from your side will be appreciated.

Many thanks and kind regards,

Tesnim
